rationale_overall: At cap=5, every incumbent has a Q1 2025 catalyst that keeps its thesis intact, so the disciplined call is no churn. NVDA reported Q4 FY25 revenue of $39.3B (+78% YoY) on Feb 26, 2025 with $11B of Blackwell shipped in its first quarter, the fastest product ramp in company history; the Jan 27 DeepSeek-driven 17% selloff has been re-examined as a sentiment shock rather than a demand impairment (Jensen on Feb 21 explicitly pushed back on the narrative), so this is exactly the kind of dip the profile says to ride, not trim. URA's SMR thesis remains supported by the hyperscaler-utility PPA cadence carried over from H2 2024 and NuScale's one-time $7.3M RoPower license payment in Q1. RKLB posted record Q4 2024 revenue of $132.4M with $1.85B backlog and bought a drone barge for Neutron landings; the Neutron timeline slip from mid-2025 to H2 2025 is a months-not-years delay and doesn't breach the rockets/spacecraft thesis. LNG continues to express the Russia-Ukraine geopolitical-energy wave the profile names. BOTZ retains coarse robotics exposure that the profile flags as a next wave. The quantum bucket remains absent and is a real thematic gap, but at cap=5 with five working theses, ejecting a healthy holding to chase quantum (which itself sold off hard on Jensen's Jan 7 'useful quantum is 15-30 years away' comment) would be the opposite of trim-before-crest discipline. Hold the line.
